channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin asks DOJ to clarify scope of president’s military power ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.), the top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, is urging the Department of Justice (DOJ) to rescind a number of internal opinions dealing with a president’s military powers while asking for clarification on domestic use of America’s forces.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Newsom is to 'blame' for 'apocalyptic' wildfires
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump slammed California Gov. Gavin Newsom for the wildfires raging in California, pinning blame for the fire on his environmental policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden casts doubt on his fitness to serve another four years days before term ends
item.description: President Joe Biden is casting doubt on his fitness to serve another four years in a new interview days before he leaves office after one term.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dems accused of 'stonewalling' Tulsi Gabbard confirmation after GOP demands quick hearing
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and the Trump transition team have accused Senate Intelligence Committee Vice Chairman Mark Warner of "stonewalling" her confirmation process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany's leader says all countries must respect existing borders, in a rebuttal of Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz said Wednesday that the principle of inviolability of borders applies to every country, however powerful, and suggested that expansionist comments by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump are meeting with “incomprehension” among European leaders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta ending 3rd-party fact checkers 'transformative,' but other legal issues remain, says expert
item.description: Facebook's new content moderation modeled after X's "Community Notes" has been praised by free speech advocates but is unlikely to ease legal liability for Meta.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Garland to release Smith's Jan. 6 report, hold back Mar-a-Lago report
item.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland plans to release only the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s plans to subvert the transfer of power after his loss in the 2020 election, holding back on sharing the Mar-a-Lago report while the president-elect's two co-defendants
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: AG Merrick Garland intends to release Special Counsel Jack Smith report on Trump election case
item.description: U.S. Attorney General Merrick Garland intends to release Special Counsel Jack Smith's report on the 2020 election interference case, but not the classified documents case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department says it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep the rest of the report under wraps for now.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From 'blackface' bombshell to applauding a Nazi: Trudeau's 5 biggest blunders as PM
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced he will step down as the nation's head of government. Fox Digital compiled his five biggest blunders over his years in the office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland and the Panama Canal
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disgraced former US Rep. George Santos seeks to delay fraud sentencing to make more podcast episodes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Disgraced former congressman George Santos has asked a New York judge to delay his sentencing on federal fraud charges until the summer so he can pay off more than half a million dollars in fines by making more episodes of his recently launched podcast “Pants on Fire.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Warren presses Hegseth to answer questions about his past actions and statements
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sen. Elizabeth Warren is pressing Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for defense secretary, to answer additional questions about his past actions and statements before next Tuesday's confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Harris will travel to Asia, Mideast and Europe during her final week in office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris plans to close out her term with an around-the-world trip making stops in Singapore, Bahrain and Germany, her office said. The trip, which is scheduled to last from Jan. 13 to Jan. 17, will be a final opportunity for Harris to address U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Republicans pushing to make voter ID a constitutional requirement
item.description: Other states have taken similar steps in recent years to put voter ID requirements in the constitution. Voters approved it in Mississippi in 2011 and North Carolina voters in 2018, while Minnesota voters rejected it in 2012.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. leaders honor former President Jimmy Carter legacy of service and citizenship
item.description: Carter, who died Dec. 29 at age 100, was transported to the Capitol by a horse-drawn cassion after arriving from Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: British prime minister condemns ‘lies and misinformation’ after Musk attacks
item.description: Elon Musk has criticized Keir Starmer, called for new elections in the U.K. and voiced support for far-right figures in the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will be sentenced Jan. 10 in New York case, days before his inauguration
item.description: In a decision Friday, New York Judge Juan Merchan noted that his inclination was to not impose a sentence of incarceration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected House speaker vote after dramatic floor vote
item.description: Republican Mike Johnson has won re-election to House speakership on first-round voting. He pushed past GOP holdout and got a boost from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tougher penalties, AI technology at center of Gov. Tim Walz plan to combat fraud
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz rolled out a proposal and signed an executive order to step up efforts to combat fraud in government programs. Some of it would require legislative action.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Biden to award Medal of Honor to Korean and Vietnam War veterans
item.description: The Medal of Honor, the United States’ highest military decoration, is awarded to service members who display exceptional valor in combat.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Schapitl, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota prosecutor wants to keep word ‘senator’ out of Nicole Mitchell trial
item.description: A high-profile trial is due to start this month over a state lawmaker’s burglary arrest. A prosecutor wants to keep Nicole Mitchell’s “senator” title away from the jury.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News
